sources.notes: Statement and constraints: construct a bounded number of tree operations, each selecting one, two, or three vertices.
Official editorial confirms the bipartite coloring and component/path decomposition style construction.
Chinese solution cross-checks the tree-DP / constructive path-decomposition view.

annotation.summary: Given a tree, output a sequence of allowed vertex-set operations within floor(5n/4) operations that satisfies the statement's target condition.
annotation.constraints: sum n <= 200000; the operation count bound is tight enough to require a linear constructive proof.
annotation.core_idea: Use the tree's bipartition and choose the smaller color class as the special side. A baseline operation handles every vertex, then deleting the special color decomposes the other color into small path-like components that can be fixed by local extra operations. The component/path accounting keeps the number of extra operations within n/4, giving the floor(5n/4) bound.
tricks: Bipartition the tree and choose the smaller color class.
Delete one side to decompose the rest into manageable components.
Use local three-vertex operations to repair paths while preserving the global operation bound.

solution_variants.name: bipartite-color-path-decomposition
solution_variants.summary: Color the tree, choose the smaller side, emit baseline operations, and add local operations for the remaining components.

solution_variants.name: tree-dp-classification
solution_variants.summary: Equivalent implementation view using DFS/DP to classify endpoints and special vertices before outputting the same linear construction.

tags.evidence: The task requires explicitly outputting an operation sequence and proving it respects a global count bound.
The construction relies on tree bipartition and the structure of components after removing one color class.
After removing selected-color vertices, the remaining vertices are handled component by component and the operation bound is analyzed by components.
Implementation uses DFS to color, traverse components, and output local operations.
A tree-DP classification of endpoints and special vertices is an equivalent way to implement the construction.
